name: modelspec

metadata.name: modelspec
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 (LGPLv3)
metadata.summary: A common JSON/YAML based format for compact model specification

documentation:
# Modelspec

[![Continuous builds](https://github.com/ModECI/modelspec/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/ModECI/modelspec/actions/workflows/ci.yml) [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/modelspec)](https://pypi.org/project/modelspec/)

Functionality for specifying the allowed structure of models, facilitating the creation of APIs in Python for handling the models & enabling automatic serialization of instances of them (e.g. in JSON and YAML format).

This package is being used by [NeuroMLlite](https://github.com/NeuroML/NeuroMLlite) & [MDF](https://github.com/ModECI/MDF).

For an example of the use of this package see [here](examples/README.md).

functions.name: modelspec.utils.load_json
documentation:
Load a generic JSON file

Args:
    filename: The name of the JSON file to load

functions.name: modelspec.utils.load_yaml
documentation:
Load a generic YAML file

Args:
    filename: The name of the YAML file to load

functions.name: modelspec.utils.load_bson
documentation:
Load a generic BSON file

Args:
    filename: The name of the BSON file to load

functions.name: modelspec.utils.load_xml
documentation:
Load a generic XML file.

Args:
    filename: The name of the XML file to load.

functions.name: modelspec.utils.elementtree_element_to_dict
documentation:
This convert an ElementTree element to a dictionary.

Args:
    element: The ElementTree element to convert.

Returns:
    The converted dictionary.

functions.name: modelspec.utils.convert_xml_dict_values
documentation:
This recursively converts values to their actual types.

Args:
    value: The value to be converted.

Returns:
    The converted value with its actual data type.

functions.name: modelspec.utils.save_to_xml_file
documentation:
This saves a dictionary to an XML file.

Args:
    info_dict (dict): The dictionary containing the data to be saved.
    filename (str): The name of the file to save the XML data to.
    indent (int, optional): The number of spaces used for indentation in the XML file.
                            Defaults to 4.

functions.name: modelspec.utils.build_xml_element
documentation:
This recursively builds an XML element structure from a dictionary or a list.

Args:
    parent: The parent XML element to attach the new element(s) to.
    data: The data to convert into XML elements.

Returns:
    Parent

functions.name: modelspec.utils.locate_file
documentation: Utility method for finding full path to a filename as string

functions.name: modelspec.utils._params_info
documentation: Short info on names, values and types in parameter list

functions.name: modelspec.utils.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluate a general string like expression (e.g. "2 * weight") using a dict
of parameters (e.g. {'weight':10}). Returns floats, ints, etc. if that's what's
given in expr

Args:
    expr: The expression to convert
    parameters: A dict of the parameters which can be substituted in to the expression
    rng: The random number generator to use
    array_format: numpy or tensorflow
    verbose: Print the calculations
    cast_to_int: return an int for float/string values if castable

functions.name: modelspec.base_types.print_
documentation: Print a message preceded by modelspec, only if print_it=True

functions.name: modelspec.base_types.print_v
documentation: Print a message preceded by modelspec always

functions.name: modelspec.base_types._unstructure_value_expr
documentation: Handle unstructuring of value expressions from JSON
Below are a set of structure and unstructure hooks needed to help cattr serialize and deserialize from JSON the
non-standard things.

functions.name: modelspec.base_types._structure_value_expr
documentation: Handle re-structuring of value expressions from JSON

functions.name: modelspec.base_types._base_unstruct_hook_factory
documentation: Generate an unstructure hook for the a class that inherits from Base
Setup a hook factory for any Base class so we don't serialize attributes whose
current value is the default value.

functions.name: modelspec.base_types._base_struct_hook_factory
documentation:
This is a very simple modification to cattr.Converter.structure_attrs_fromdict. The only
difference is that we give the id value a default value of empty string when structuring.
This is because objects with id are serialized without id when they are stored in a list.
The id is stored in the key of a dictionary.
Most of the code below is needed to handle serialization and deserialization of lists of
Base objects that have ids as dicts where the keys are the ids.

functions.name: modelspec.base_types._unstructure_list_base
documentation: Serialize list of Base objects as dicts if all of their elements have ids
Register the structure and unstructure hooks for collections of Base classes.

functions.name: modelspec.base_types._structure_list_base
documentation: Deserialize list of dict of Base objects as a list if all of their elements have ids

functions.name: modelspec.base_types._is_list_base
documentation:
Check if a class is a list of Base objects. These will be serialized as dicts if the underlying class has an id
attribute.

classes.name: modelspec.base_types.EvaluableExpression
documentation:
EvaluableExpression is a string that can be evaluated to a value during modelspec execution. This class inherits from
str, so it can be used as a string.

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.to_json_file
documentation:
Convert the modelspec model to JSON format and save to a file.

> :::{note}
> JSON is standard file format uses human-readable text to store and transmit data objects consisting of
>
> : attribute–value pairs and arrays
> :::

Args:

: filename: The name of the file to save. If None, use {code}`f"{self.id}.json"`
  include_metadata: Contains contact information, citations, acknowledgements, pointers to sample data,

  > benchmark results, and environments in which the specified model was originally implemented

Returns:

: The name of the generated JSON file

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.to_bson_file
documentation:
Convert modelspec format to bson format

Args:
    filename: File in modelspec format (Filename extension: .bson )
    include_metadata: Contains contact information, citations, acknowledgements, pointers to sample data,
                      benchmark results, and environments in which the specified model was originally implemented
Returns:
    The name of the generated bson file

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.to_yaml
documentation:
Convert the Base object to a YAML dictionary representation.

Args:
    include_metadata: Should metadata be included in the YAML output?

Returns:
    A YAML string representation of the object.

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.to_yaml_file
documentation:
Convert modelspec format to yaml format

Args:

: filename: File in modelspec format (Filename extension: .yaml). If None, use {code}`f"{self.id}.yaml"`
  include_metadata: Contains contact information, citations, acknowledgements, pointers to sample data,

  > benchmark results, and environments in which the specified model was originally implemented

Returns:

: The name of the generated yaml file

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.from_file
documentation:
Create a {class}`.Base` from its representation stored in a file. Auto-detect the correct deserialization code
based on file extension. Currently supported formats are; JSON(.json) and YAML (.yaml or .yml)

Args:

: filename: The name of the file to load.

Returns:

: An modelspec {class}`.Base` for this file.

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.from_json_file
documentation:
Create a {class}`.Base` from its JSON representation stored in a file.

Args:

: filename: The file from which to load the JSON data.

Returns:

: An modelspec {class}`.Base` for this JSON

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.from_bson_file
documentation:
Create a {class}`.Base` from its BSON representation stored in a file.

Args:

: filename: The file from which to load the BSON data.

Returns:

: An modelspec {class}`.Base` for this BSON

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.from_yaml_file
documentation:
Create a {class}`.Base` from its YAML representation stored in a file.

Args:

: filename: The file from which to load the YAML data.

Returns:

: An modelspec {class}`.Base` for this YAML

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.from_xml_file
documentation:
Create a Base from its XML representation stored in a file.

Args:
    filename: The file from which to load the XML data.

Returns:
    A modelspec Base for this XML.

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.get_child
documentation:
Get a child object from the model specification.

Args:
    id: The unique string identifier of the child object.
    type_: The type of the child object. Must be an attribute name that contains a list of child objects with
        id attributes..

Returns:
    The child object. If the child object is not found, None is returned.

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.definition
documentation:
The definition of the model.

Returns:
    The definition of the model.

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.allowed_fields
documentation:
Get the list of allowed fields for this model.

Returns:
    The allowed fields are returned as dict keyed by field name
        and with values of tuple with (field_description, field_type).

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.allowed_children
documentation:
Get the list of allowed children for this model. Children are defined as
any attribute of this object that is a collection of objects that inherits from {class}`Base`.

Returns:
: The allowed children are returned as dict keyed attribute name of the collection

  : and with values of tuple with (description, type).

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base._parse_definition
documentation:
Parse the definition of the model from its doc string.

Returns:
    The definition of the model.

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base._parse_allowed_fields
documentation:
Get the list of allowed fields for this model.

Returns:
    The allowed fields are returned as dict keyed by field name
        and with values of tuple with (field_description, field_type).

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base._is_child_field
documentation:
Check if a field is a child field. A child field is an attribute that is a collection of objects
that inherits from {class}`Base`.

Args:

: field_name: The name of the field to check.

Returns:

: True if the field is a child field, False otherwise.

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base._parse_allowed_children
documentation:
Get the list of allowed children for this model. Children are defined as
any attribute of this object that is a collection of objects that inherits from {class}`Base`.

Returns:
: The allowed children are returned as dict keyed attribute name of the collection

  : and with values of tuple with (description, type).

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base._is_evaluable_expression
documentation: Helper method to check if a value is of type EvaluableExpressions

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base._type_to_str
documentation:
Convert a Python type (or type annotation) (from {code}`typing`) to a prettier format. Also handles normal types as well.

Args:

: [type]: The type or type annotation to convert.

Returns:

: A string with a prettier format of the type annotation.

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base.generate_documentation
documentation:
Generate documentation for the modelspec object.

Args:
    format: The format to generate the documentation in. Currently supported formats are: ['markdown', 'dict']

methods.name: modelspec.base_types.Base._cls_generate_documentation
documentation:
Generate documentation for the modelspec object.

Args:
    format: The format to generate the documentation in. Currently supported formats are: ['markdown', 'dict']
Base class for all object in a model specification. Any object should inherit from this class.
